title: Knautia travnicensis

intro: Knautia transylvanica, also known as Transylvanian scabiosa or Knautia travnicensis, is a perennial plant belonging to the caper family (Caprifoliaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, including wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Knautia transylvanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Knautia transylvanica is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including gastrointestinal problems and skin irritations.
